summary: Repository-wide formatting PR that introduced a Makefile `format` target, adjusted `.clang-format` so automated formatting would not perturb binary output, protected inline assembly blocks with `// clang-format off`, and applied the resulting formatting across existing source and include files.
what_changed: Added a `format` target to the Makefile.
Modified `.clang-format` to avoid changes that could affect binary output.
Disabled include sorting in the clang-format configuration.
Disabled assert formatting in the clang-format configuration.
Added `// clang-format off` annotations to inline assembly functions.
Applied the new formatting style to all existing source and include files.
smart_moves: Treated formatting as decomp-sensitive tooling rather than ordinary style cleanup by explicitly configuring clang-format not to change binary output.
Disabled include sorting, which is important in a decompilation project because header/include ordering can affect declarations, macros, and sometimes generated code.
Protected inline assembly with `// clang-format off`, avoiding formatter edits to code that is especially fragile for matching and correctness.
Added a Makefile target so future contributors can run the same formatter consistently instead of relying on manual style choices.
decomp_lessons: Repository-wide formatting is safest when the formatter configuration is constrained to preserve matching-sensitive constructs.
Do not allow automated formatters to reorder includes in decomp work unless the effect on all translation units is known.
Inline assembly should generally be fenced off from clang-format because whitespace, labels, constraints, or line structure may be semantically or matching sensitive.
A shared `make format`-style target helps normalize future cleanup without making contributors guess the approved clang-format invocation.
Large formatting-only PRs should clearly document the formatter exceptions that protect binary matching.
naming_conventions: Inline assembly formatting was controlled using standard `// clang-format off` comments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Configured `.clang-format` with no include sorting to reduce risk of binary-output changes.
Configured `.clang-format` with no assert formatting according to the PR description.
Added `// clang-format off` around inline asm functions to prevent formatter-induced changes in assembly-heavy code.
Applied formatting only after adding protections intended to preserve binary output.
review_feedback: No review comment details were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review recorded, but no review comments or examples were included in the dump.
follow_up_queries: Find the exact `.clang-format` options changed in PR 628.
